# DB Pro

> DB Pro is a modern database management tool for developers. It provides a unified interface for working with multiple databases including PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Microsoft SQL Server, and MongoDB.

## Quick Facts

- Website: https://dbpro.app
- Platforms: macOS, Windows, Linux
- Pricing: Free plan available, $9.99/month, or $99 one-time purchase for lifetime license
- Support: Discord (https://discord.gg/FKKF65msZY)

## Operating System Support

- macOS (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Windows (64-bit)
- Linux (64-bit, AppImage and .deb)

## Supported Databases

- PostgreSQL
- MySQL
- SQLite
- Microsoft SQL Server (MSSQL)
- MongoDB
- Supabase (built-in support)
- Neon (built-in support)
- Turso / libSQL (built-in support)
- PlanetScale (built-in support)

## Key Features

- SQL Editor: Syntax highlighting, autocomplete, multi-tab queries, query history
- Data Browser: View, filter, sort, and edit rows with inline editing
- Schema Explorer: Visual navigation of tables, views, indexes, and relationships
- Import/Export: CSV and JSON support with column mapping
- SSH Tunneling: Secure connections to remote databases
- Credential Storage: Integration with system keychain (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager)
- Saved Queries: Save and version queries for reuse

## Free Plan Includes

- Full SQL editor
- Data browser
- Schema explorer
- Query history
- Unlimited local database connections
- No time limits or trial restrictions

## Comparisons

- vs DataGrip: $99 lifetime vs $199/year subscription. Built-in support for Supabase, Neon, Turso, PlanetScale.
- vs TablePlus: $99 lifetime vs $89 lifetime. Similar feature set, DB Pro has built-in cloud database provider support.
- vs Navicat: $99 lifetime for all databases vs $199-699 per database type.
- vs DBeaver: Cleaner UI, more streamlined experience. DBeaver is free/open source with more features.
- vs Sequel Pro: DB Pro is actively maintained, cross-platform, supports more databases.
- vs phpMyAdmin: Desktop app, no web server required, supports multiple database types.

## Links

- Download: https://dbpro.app/download
- Pricing: https://dbpro.app/#pricing
- Documentation: https://dbpro.app/help
- Blog: https://dbpro.app/blog
- FAQ: https://dbpro.app/faq
